description:
You are a web-developer who just inherited a very small “legacy-analytics” project.  
    Your team needs proof that the old tracking script still runs without errors.

    Existing project folder:
      /home/user/legacy-analytics

    Already inside that folder is one script:
      /home/user/legacy-analytics/tracker.py   ← prints a single line: legacy-analytics OK

    Your job in the terminal:

    1. Change into /home/user/legacy-analytics.  
    2. Execute the Python script (use the default python3 interpreter that ships with the container).  
    3. Capture the script’s *standard output* into a new log file named:
         /home/user/legacy-analytics/verification.log
    4. The log file must contain **exactly one line** (including the trailing newline):
         legacy-analytics OK
       No extra spaces before/after the text, no blank lines.
    5. Display the contents of verification.log in the terminal so a reviewer can visually confirm success.

    The automated tests will check:
      • That /home/user/legacy-analytics/verification.log exists.  
      • That the file’s contents are exactly: legacy-analytics␠OK⏎ (where ⏎ is a single newline).
truth:
Directory that already exists when the task starts:
      /home/user/legacy-analytics   (owner: user, perms: 755)

    Pre-existing file inside the directory:
      Path: /home/user/legacy-analytics/tracker.py
      Mode: 755
      Contents (exact, LF line endings):
        #!/usr/bin/env python3
        print("legacy-analytics OK")

    Expected artefact after agent finishes:
      Path: /home/user/legacy-analytics/verification.log
      Mode: 644 (any user-readable)
      Exact contents (including trailing newline):
        legacy-analytics OK\n
